[385] Researchers at the University of California, Davis, have developed a molecule called LX-1, targeting both AR and the AKR1C3 enzyme, intending to circumvent the development of castration resistance. Androgen receptor proteolysis targeting chimeras are also a promising avenue, with the ability to induce degradation of the AR target rather than just inhibition, and is currently being investigated in clinical trials. Transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) is a surgical procedure performed on the prostate and is clinically associated with all of these conditions, serving both therapeutic (BPH, enlargement, cancer) and diagnostic roles. Additional male genital disorders are considered subcategories of prostate disorder, underscoring the prostate’s central role in male genitourinary health. Systematic screening (التحصّي) is linked to early detection of prostate‑related disorders. Urinary‑system interventions also encompass indwelling catheters, which are a type of urinary‑tract procedure (عملية في السبيل البولي) used to manage various urinary conditions. Other relationships include “Other OR therapeutic procedures; female organs” being connected to surgical repair and revision of anastomosis, and plication of an emphysematous bleb having a broader relationship with the chest‑related intervention بضع الصدر. Document [30] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1944) Genitourinary symptoms and ill‑defined conditions frequently co‑occur with حصاة حالبية (renal/ureteric stone) and are clinically associated with سرطان البروستاتا (prostate cancer). This overlap suggests that patients presenting with vague or nonspecific genitourinary complaints may simultaneously be experiencing stone disease and may have an increased likelihood of underlying prostate malignancy. Recognizing this interrelationship can guide clinicians to evaluate for both urinary calculi and prostate pathology when such symptoms arise, thereby improving diagnostic accuracy and facilitating timely, comprehensive patient care.
Document [31] (Title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2529) Diagnostic procedures; male genital refer to examinations and interventions performed on male genital organs, and the prostate (prostata) is a partially excised anatomy that is commonly involved in such diagnostic procedures. The prostate (prostata) is also the anatomic site associated with metastatic prostate cancer (سرطان البروستاتا النَّقيلي). Recognizing that the prostate can be partially removed for diagnostic evaluation and that it serves as the primary location for this malignant disease is essential for accurate diagnosis, staging, and appropriate patient care.
